The intent action `com.slumdog88.dictationkeyboardai.UPDATE_BUBBLE_APPEARANCE (used to send a broadcast)` matches the intent filter of a non-exported receiver, registered via a call to `Context.registerReceiver`, or similar. If you are trying to invoke this specific receiver via the action then you should use `Intent.setPackage(<APPLICATION_ID>)`.

issue.message:
Hardcoded string "Hey, thank you for downloading and installing WonderWhisper. In short, this project was born because I love AI dictation apps and been using them on my Mac for quite some time with the likes of SuperWhisper, Voicink, WisprFlow, and Talktastic. However, I found that on Android, nothing quite existed that fulfilled the same need as these apps on desktop. Whilst there was some that existed, I didn't like the way they were implemented. It felt outdated and unconfigurable and clunky.

So I set out to create a version of what we have on desktop but on Android with a very simple and easy way to action voice dictation without the need to use a dedicated keyboard. Wonder Whisper was born.

It's still very early and at the moment it's a hobby development. Please provide any feedback that you have. I am very fast to try out new ideas and implement. As a new developer, I'm just having fun.

The best way to keep up to date on the changes being made to the app or even provide feedback is please use the newly created Reddit community where I'd be happy to interact with you directly.

Cheers fam and happy dictating!", should use `@string` resource
issue.errorLine1:
android:text="Hey, thank you for downloading and installing WonderWhisper. In short, this project was born because I love AI dictation apps and been using them on my Mac for quite some time with the likes of SuperWhisper, Voicink, WisprFlow, and Talktastic. However, I found that on Android, nothing quite existed that fulfilled the same need as these apps on desktop. Whilst there was some that existed, I didn't like the way they were implemented. It felt outdated and unconfigurable and clunky.

So I set out to create a version of what we have on desktop but on Android with a very simple and easy way to action voice dictation without the need to use a dedicated keyboard. Wonder Whisper was born.

It's still very early and at the moment it's a hobby development. Please provide any feedback that you have. I am very fast to try out new ideas and implement. As a new developer, I'm just having fun.

The best way to keep up to date on the changes being made to the app or even provide feedback is please use the newly created Reddit community where I'd be happy to interact with you directly.

Cheers fam and happy dictating!"

issue.message: Hardcoded string "WHY WE NEED ACCESSIBILITY PERMISSION:", should use `@string` resource

Hardcoded string "WonderWhisper uses the AccessibilityServices API EXCLUSIVELY to:

• INSERT DICTATED TEXT into any app (Messages, Email, Notes, etc.)
• READ SELECTED TEXT to provide context for AI commands
• DETECT ACTIVE TEXT FIELDS to show the dictation bubble

This permission allows WonderWhisper to work as a system-wide dictation tool across all your apps.", should use `@string` resource
issue.errorLine1:
android:text="WonderWhisper uses the AccessibilityServices API EXCLUSIVELY to:

• INSERT DICTATED TEXT into any app (Messages, Email, Notes, etc.)
• READ SELECTED TEXT to provide context for AI commands
• DETECT ACTIVE TEXT FIELDS to show the dictation bubble

This permission allows WonderWhisper to work as a system-wide dictation tool across all your apps."

issue.message: Hardcoded string "HOW YOUR DATA IS USED:", should use `@string` resource

Hardcoded string "• VOICE RECORDINGS: Temporarily processed for transcription, then immediately deleted
• SELECTED TEXT: Used only for AI command context, not stored permanently
• APP CONTEXT: Used to improve dictation accuracy, not tracked or stored
• TRANSCRIPTION LOGS: Stored locally on your device only, you control deletion

NO DATA IS SENT TO OUR SERVERS. All processing uses your own API keys with OpenAI/AssemblyAI/etc.", should use `@string` resource
issue.errorLine1:
android:text="• VOICE RECORDINGS: Temporarily processed for transcription, then immediately deleted
• SELECTED TEXT: Used only for AI command context, not stored permanently
• APP CONTEXT: Used to improve dictation accuracy, not tracked or stored
• TRANSCRIPTION LOGS: Stored locally on your device only, you control deletion

NO DATA IS SENT TO OUR SERVERS. All processing uses your own API keys with OpenAI/AssemblyAI/etc."

issue.message: Hardcoded string "📖 HOW IT WORKS", should use `@string` resource

Hardcoded string "1. Tap in any text field → A floating bubble appears automatically
2. Tap the microphone in the bubble to start recording
3. Speak clearly into your device
4. Tap the microphone again to finish recording
5. Your text appears automatically!

🤖 KEYBOARD-AWARE MODE (Default):
• Bubble appears when you open a text field and keyboard shows
• Bubble disappears when you swipe back to home screen
• Quick toggle: Swipe notifications down and back up to show/hide bubble

⚙️ SMART BUBBLE CONTROLS:
• 🎤 TAP: Start/stop recording
• ⏳ LONG PRESS (when not recording): Hide the bubble
• ❌ LONG PRESS (while recording): Cancel recording
• 📱 Use notification controls to show/hide the bubble manually
• 🏠 Swipe to home screen to automatically dismiss the bubble
• 📱 Swipe notifications panel to quickly toggle bubble visibility

💡 NEW: Context-sensitive long press replaces separate buttons!", should use `@string` resource
issue.errorLine1:
android:text="1. Tap in any text field → A floating bubble appears automatically
2. Tap the microphone in the bubble to start recording
3. Speak clearly into your device
4. Tap the microphone again to finish recording
5. Your text appears automatically!

🤖 KEYBOARD-AWARE MODE (Default):
• Bubble appears when you open a text field and keyboard shows
• Bubble disappears when you swipe back to home screen
• Quick toggle: Swipe notifications down and back up to show/hide bubble

⚙️ SMART BUBBLE CONTROLS:
• 🎤 TAP: Start/stop recording
• ⏳ LONG PRESS (when not recording): Hide the bubble
• ❌ LONG PRESS (while recording): Cancel recording
• 📱 Use notification controls to show/hide the bubble manually
• 🏠 Swipe to home screen to automatically dismiss the bubble
• 📱 Swipe notifications panel to quickly toggle bubble visibility

💡 NEW: Context-sensitive long press replaces separate buttons!"

issue.message: Hardcoded string "🤖 DICTATION VS AI PROCESSING", should use `@string` resource

Hardcoded string "📝 DICTATION MODE (Raw):
• Mirrors your exact words, including filler words
• Example: 'Um, hello there, uh, how are you?'

🧠 AI PROCESSING (Smart):
• Cleans up filler words (um, uh, like)
• Fixes grammar and adds punctuation
• Restructures for better readability
• Uses screen context for accuracy
• Example: 'Hello there, how are you?'", should use `@string` resource
issue.errorLine1:
android:text="📝 DICTATION MODE (Raw):
• Mirrors your exact words, including filler words
• Example: 'Um, hello there, uh, how are you?'

🧠 AI PROCESSING (Smart):
• Cleans up filler words (um, uh, like)
• Fixes grammar and adds punctuation
• Restructures for better readability
• Uses screen context for accuracy
• Example: 'Hello there, how are you?'"

issue.message: Hardcoded string "⚡ COMMAND MODE", should use `@string` resource

• 'command, what is the population of America?'

android:text="Start your dictation with 'command' to interact with AI:

💡 Transform Text:
• 'command, reformat my clipboard text'
• 'command, summarize the selected text'
• 'command, make this more professional'

❓ Ask Questions:
• 'command, what is the population of America?'
• 'command, how do I cook pasta?'

🎯 The magic word is 'command' at the very beginning!"
